title: Comparative risk of hypophosphatemia following intravenous iron preparations
author: Ioannis Bellos
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bebyjapw

steps.title: Review title
steps.instruction: Review title: Comparative risk of hypophosphatemia following the administration of intravenous iron preparations: a network meta-analysis

steps.title: Review question
steps.instruction: Review question: To compare the incidence of hypophosphatemia following the intravenous administration of different iron preparations (ferric carboxymaltose, ferumoxytol, iron isomaltoside, iron sucrose and iron dextran).

steps.title: Searches
steps.instruction: Searches: No date or language restrictions will be applied. MEDLINE, Scopus, CENTRAL, Web of Science, ClinicalTrials.gov and Google Scholar databases will be systematically searched from inception. Literature search will include the terms: hypophosphatemia, phosphorus, iron, ferric, ferrous, carboxymaltose, ferumoxytol, isomaltoside, sucrose, dextran.

steps.title: Participants/population Inclusion criteria
steps.instruction: Participants/population Inclusion criteria: patients receiving any intravenous iron preparation for any indication (e.g. iron-deficiency, chronic kidney disease, inflammatory bowel disease or hemorrhage) Exclusion criteria: Administration of oral iron, intravenous iron preparation not specified, no definition of hypophosphatemia

steps.title: Intervention(s), exposure(s)
steps.instruction: Intervention(s), exposure(s): Administration of any iron preparation (ferric carboxymaltose, ferumoxytol, iron isomaltoside, iron sucrose and iron dextran).

steps.title: Types of study to be included...
steps.instruction: Types of study to be included Randomized controlled trials (RCTs) will be held eligible. Cohort, case-control or cross-sectional studies, case reports, case series, animal studies, review articles and in vitro studies will be excluded.

steps.title: Main outcome
steps.instruction: Main outcome: Incidence of hypophosphatemia, defined as serum phosphorus below 2.0 mg/dl.

steps.title: Additional outcome(s)
steps.instruction: Additional outcome(s): Incidence of severe hypophosphatemia (serum phosphorus below 1.3 mg/dl) and persistent hypophosphatemia.

steps.title: Data extraction (selection and coding)
steps.instruction: Data extraction (selection and coding): RCTs will be held eligible if they report the risk of hypophosphatemia among patients treated with intravenous iron. The following data will be extracted from each study: name of first author, date of publication, country, study design, inclusion and exclusion criteria, clinical setting, indication for iron treatment, presence of chronic kidney disease, definition of hypophosphatemia, iron dose, number of patients and patients’ baseline characteristics (e.g. age, gender, renal function, comorbidities).

steps.title: Risk of bias (quality) assessment
steps.instruction: Risk of bias (quality) assessment: All studies will be assessed using the Cochrane risk of bias tool for randomized controlled trials, taking into account the domains of randomization, blinding, allocation concealment, withdrawals and dropouts.

steps.title: Strategy for data synthesis
steps.instruction: Strategy for data synthesis: Network meta-analysis is planned to be conducted in order to simultaneously compare hypophosphatemia risk for all intravenous iron preparations. League tables, posterior density plots and cumulative ranking curves will be constructed. Consistency will be assessed both globally and locally, while transitivity will be tested by evaluating the distributions of potential confounding factors across studies classified by comparison.

steps.title: Analysis of subgroups or subsets
steps.instruction: Analysis of subgroups or subsets: Subgrouping is planned on the basis of treatment indication and presence of chronic kidney disease.
